title: Self Join
content:
### Self Join

A self join is a regular join where a table is joined with itself. It is used to compare rows within the same table or find hierarchical relationships.

---

### Self Join Syntax

```sql
SELECT a.columns, b.columns
FROM table a
JOIN table b ON a.column = b.column;
```

Table aliases are required in a self join because you reference the same table twice.

### Employee-Manager Example

```sql
-- Find each employee and their manager's name
SELECT e.name AS employee,
       m.name AS manager
FROM employees e
LEFT JOIN employees m ON e.manager_id = m.id;
```

### Finding Pairs

```sql
-- Find employees who work in the same department
SELECT a.name AS employee1,
       b.name AS employee2,
       a.department
FROM employees a
JOIN employees b ON a.department = b.department
    AND a.id < b.id  -- Avoid duplicates and self-pairs
ORDER BY a.department;
```

### Comparing Values Within a Table

```sql
-- Find products with prices close to each other
SELECT a.name AS product_a,
       b.name AS product_b,
       a.price AS price_a,
       b.price AS price_b
FROM products a
JOIN products b ON ABS(a.price - b.price) < 10
    AND a.id < b.id
ORDER BY a.price;
```

### Hierarchical Data

Self joins are essential for hierarchical data stored in a single table:

| id | name | parent_id |
|----|------|-----------|
| 1 | CEO | NULL |
| 2 | VP Engineering | 1 |
| 3 | VP Marketing | 1 |
| 4 | Engineering Manager | 2 |

```sql
SELECT child.name AS employee,
       parent.name AS reports_to
FROM employees child
LEFT JOIN employees parent ON child.parent_id = parent.id;
```

> **Key Point:** Self joins require table aliases to distinguish between the two instances of the same table.
codeExample:
SELECT e1.name AS employee,
       e2.name AS colleague,
       e1.department
FROM employees e1
JOIN employees e2 ON e1.department = e2.department
    AND e1.id < e2.id;

quiz.question: What is required when performing a self join?
Self joins are commonly used for:
options: Finding hierarchical relationships within a single table
